answers: Organic farming avoids synthetic pesticides and fertilizers. Uses natural methods like composting, crop rotation, and biological pest control.
Benefits: healthier food, better soil, less pollution, and premium prices for your produce. Growing demand worldwide.
Start organic farming: begin with composting, learn about companion planting, get organic certification, and connect with local organic markets.
Organic vs conventional: organic is chemical-free, better for environment, but lower yield initially. Long-term soil health is much better.
